references.reason: Kaguya's route begins at a bamboo gate where an impossible request has a visible exit.
A tablet makes Kaguya's five requests material without treating them as compulsory tests.
The open gate makes refusal and continued presence visible in the route's first scene.
Kaguya's first route journal records that a request remains safe only when it has an exit.
Kaguya chooses how to host a reception without having to perform a fixed princess persona.
The guest list separates a chosen welcome from a compulsory performance.
An unset chair keeps the reception able to change with its guests.
Kaguya records hospitality as a choice rather than an obligation of rank.
Kaguya lets a one-life board game end in a quiet room rather than resetting its outcome.
The board has one authored game and no compelled replay.
A returned piece visibly marks that the finished game is not rewound.
Kaguya records finite loss without turning it into punishment or a lesson owed.
A veranda at dawn makes the limited duration of a shared morning visible without demanding an eternal answer.
Tea cooling on the veranda lets the route name a near, finite appointment rather than promise equivalent lifespans.
An unmarked calendar lets Kaguya and the protagonist make one honest plan without pretending time is owned.
Kaguya records that a mortal future is neither a debt nor a reason to make a false eternal promise.
A bamboo clearing after Mokou's arrival keeps the immortal rivalry in public space rather than making it a private possession test.
Singed bamboo records that the rivals' old conflict has physical consequences without treating violence as romance.
An unopened flask gives Mokou room to leave without participating in Eientei's hospitality.
The journal records that neither immortal may turn another person's finite life into a token in their rivalry.
The Archive offers a preserved evening beside a shutter Kaguya can choose to open to dawn.
The lens offers a perfect preserved evening without claiming that preservation is presence.
The shutter makes Kaguya’s decision to admit daylight visible and hers.
Kaguya records an evening kept by living through its end, not by letting the Archive repeat it.
